summary: The docs module is the authoritative source of truth for how the anchor project is built and evolved. It consists of three interlocking documents: architecture.md defines the canonical system design and invariants that all code must satisfy; phase-prompts.md contains the complete ordered build plan, decomposed into stories with acceptance criteria and instructions that drive the builder agent; checkpoints.md defines the phase-boundary gates — tagged milestones that require the full build gate, security audit, and intent review sequence to pass before the next phase begins. Together these documents act as a persistent contract between the human developer and the AI agents: architecture.md is the what-and-why, phase-prompts.md is the step-by-step how, and checkpoints.md is the when-is-it-done.
business_rules: architecture.md must be read before any build or review task — it is the first input to every agent session and overrides any agent default behavior.
phase-prompts.md must be read in full by the build orchestrator before starting any story — no story may begin without the orchestrator having the complete phase context.
Each story in phase-prompts.md must have an explicit acceptance criterion; a story without one may not be started.
Checkpoints must only be tagged after all stories in the phase pass the full sequence: build gate, security audit, and intent review. Partial phase completion does not qualify.
architecture.md must reflect the actual implemented module structure, data flow, layer rules, and protected files at all times — it is a live spec, not aspirational design.

The canonical spec format defined in architecture.md (module, summary, business_rules, non_negotiables, tradeoffs, conflicts, lineage) is binding on all spec.json files produced by or consumed by the system.
phase-prompts.md stories are executed in order within a phase; a later story may not be started if an earlier story in the same phase has not passed its acceptance criterion.
checkpoints.md records the git tag command for each checkpoint so the tagging step is reproducible and unambiguous.
Developer Action gates in phase-prompts.md (marked with the gear symbol) require human confirmation before the build proceeds to the next phase — they are not skippable by the builder agent.
non_negotiables: architecture.md must never be allowed to drift from the actual implementation — it is the source of truth and a diverged architecture doc is a CRITICAL integrity failure.
The hook architecture non-negotiable documented in architecture.md (hooks are thin relays only, no API calls, no direct spec writes) must never be contradicted or softened in any revision of the doc.
The append-only invariant for spec lineage and lessons.json must be explicitly stated in architecture.md and must never be removed from it.
phase-prompts.md must never be edited to retroactively change a story's acceptance criterion after that story has been completed — doing so breaks the audit trail of what was actually built.
Checkpoints in checkpoints.md must never be removed once created; they are permanent phase-boundary records.
The protected files list in architecture.md must stay in sync with the protected files list enforced in CLAUDE.md — a discrepancy between the two is a CRITICAL configuration error.
The layer rules table in architecture.md (hooks may not import from skills; skills may not call hooks) must never be relaxed without an explicit recorded architectural decision.

tradeoffs.decision: phase-prompts.md is a flat, ordered, human-readable file rather than a structured data format (YAML/JSON).
tradeoffs.reason: The build orchestrator is an LLM agent that reads natural language instructions; a structured format would require a parser and add indirection. Human developers also need to read and author stories directly.
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Stories cannot be queried or manipulated programmatically without parsing Markdown, and the file grows linearly with no archival of completed phases.

tradeoffs.decision: architecture.md is a single flat file covering the entire system rather than per-module architecture docs.
tradeoffs.reason: A single file ensures the build orchestrator and reviewer always have the full system context in one read, reducing the risk of an agent missing a cross-module invariant.
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: The file will grow large as the system expands; future phases may need to split it, which would require updating all agent prompts that reference it.

tradeoffs.decision: checkpoints.md records only phase-level gates, not story-level completion records.
tradeoffs.reason: Story-level completion is tracked implicitly by git commit history and the build gate. Duplicating it in checkpoints.md would create a maintenance burden with no added enforcement.
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: There is no single document that gives a quick view of which individual stories are complete within a phase; that requires reading git log.

tradeoffs.decision: anchor's own docs are hand-authored, not generated from the pairmode templates in skills/pairmode/templates/docs/.
tradeoffs.reason: Anchor's docs describe a unique, evolving system. Generating them from templates would add abstraction with no benefit and risk the canonical docs becoming stale if the template diverges.
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: anchor's own architecture.md, phase-prompts.md, and checkpoints.md must be manually kept consistent with the Jinja2 templates that generate equivalent files for downstream projects.

lineage.what_changed: Specification and documentation of modular architecture, analyzing domain-admin-ui and routes modules within a 15-module product system
key_decisions: Product is organized into 15 modular units, each with discrete rule sets and constraint requirements
domain-admin-ui is a React-based administrative web console for managing domain schema and operations
Multi-tenant domain model is foundational architecture for the product
Product migrated from Rust to TypeScript backend
Database migrated from MongoDB to PostgreSQL

lineage.what_changed: Add Linux support to Anchor plugin, test the implementation, open PR to original repo, and plan integration approach for TypeScript monorepo project.
key_decisions: Manual skill execution via SKILL.md reading instead of automatic plugin-driven execution in current session
Create Linux support for Anchor plugin and contribute changes back via PR to original public repository
Update README to document Linux support
Push changes to private fork and open PR against original public repository
Install GitHub CLI (gh) for command-line PR operations
